intro: Latouria muscifera, also known as the musk-flowering Latouria, is a species of orchid native to tropical regions of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Latouria muscifera include muscone, a musk-like volatile compound, as well as other alkaloids and phenolic compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Latouria muscifera is often prepared as a decoction or made into a tincture, which are commonly used to treat coughs, fever, and skin issues.
